summary.intent: Investigate and fix HikariCP connection leak errors in OpenCue's cuebot causing frame dependencies to not be cleared after frame completion.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified (thread pool count exceeding connection pool size) and fix #3 implemented — resilient error handling around satisfyWhatDependsOn in FrameCompleteHandler. Build was interrupted before completion.

repo: OpenCue cuebot uses HikariCP with a default maximum-pool-size of 20, configured in cuebot/src/main/resources/opencue.properties
The dispatch queue (pool-4) and report queue (pool-5) are separate thread pools that both compete for the same HikariCP connection pool
satisfyWhatDependsOn is called at FrameCompleteHandler.java:285 inside handlePostFrameCompleteOperations; exceptions here can silently drop dependency clearing, leaving frames stuck in DEPEND state
The exception handler at lines 551-565 only attempts to unbook the proc on failure, without retrying or logging dependency satisfaction failures
DependManagerService.satisfyDepend is @Transactional and acquires its own connection per call

code.finding: satisfyWhatDependsOn call that can silently fail under connection exhaustion
Two stack trace hit points within handlePostFrameCompleteOperations: unbookProc at 507 and another at 457
Exception handler that only unbooks proc but does not handle dependency satisfaction failure
Runnable entry point where handlePostFrameCompleteOperations is called from thread pool
increaseReservedMemory called here also triggers @Transactional connection acquisition, contributing to pool exhaustion

workflow: cuebot requires Java 11 (temurin-11); must set JAVA_HOME=/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-11.jdk/Contents/Home before building
HikariCP's 30s leak-detection-threshold can produce false positives when threads legitimately wait for connections under pool exhaustion — the warning is a symptom, not the root cause
Plans are being saved to /Users/dtavares/.claude/plans/ for cross-session reference

friction: Java version mismatch required manual JAVA_HOME export — not documented in project setup
Gradle build caching made it hard to confirm recompilation had occurred, requiring a clean build
Build was interrupted by user before completion, so the fix could not be verified
open_items: Increase maximum-pool-size from 20 to ~46 to cover max thread count across all pools (fix #1)
Increase leak-detection-threshold from 30s to 120s to reduce false positive warnings (fix #2)
Complete and verify the FrameCompleteHandler resilient error handling change (fix #3) — build was interrupted
Add tests for the dependency-clearing failure path in FrameCompleteHandlerTests.java
Investigate whether the same silent-failure pattern exists in other places that call satisfyWhatDependsOn or satisfyDepend
